steps.title: Identify the pattern established by previous research
steps.content:
The previous finding establishes that narrow geographical range correlates with high genetic homogeneity. M. anniana currently has only a narrow range (Tamaulipas), so it has high genetic homogeneity.

The simulation expands its range to new habitats. For results to be “consistent with previous findings,” they must follow the same pattern: as range expands, genetic homogeneity decreases.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice D states that genetic homogeneity of M. anniana decreased over time—exactly what the established pattern predicts when a narrow-range species expands to a wider range.

The other choices either address different Mammillaria species (B, C) or contradict the findings by suggesting homogeneity increased (A).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
